steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says ephemeral objects ‘reveal shifts in norms, values, and concerns.’ A vocabulary-in-context question: what word best describes this role? The correct answer must mean something that points to or indicates cultural change.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'alternatives': implies a substitute or option. Ephemeral objects are not presented as alternatives to something else. ✗
  • Choice B — 'speculations': implies guesswork or uncertainty. The passage presents these objects as things that actually reveal cultural shifts, not hypothesize about them. ✗
  • Choice C — 'indices': means signs or indicators. Describing ephemeral objects as indices of cultural change matches the passage's claim that they reveal shifts in norms and values. ✓
  • Choice D — 'pretenses': implies false appearances or deception. Nothing in the passage suggests these objects are misleading. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
